info.title: uithub API
info.description: API for fetching repository contents and related information from GitHub Check openapi.json to get this in machine readable format ## OAuth 2.0 Authentication Guide uithub implements OAuth 2.0 with PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) support for secure authentication. This allows third-party applications to access the API on behalf of users. ### Quick Start **Step 1: Discover OAuth Configuration** Fetch the authorization server metadata: ``` GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server ``` **Step 2: Register Your Client (Dynamic Client Registration)** ```bash curl -X POST https://uithub.com/register \ -H "Content-Type: application/json" \ -d '{ "redirect_uris": ["https://your-app.com/callback"], "client_name": "Your App Name" }' ``` Response: ```json { "client_id": "client_abc123...", "client_secret": "secret_xyz789...", "redirect_uris": ["https://your-app.com/callback"], "client_name": "Your App Name" } ``` **Step 3: Generate PKCE Parameters** Generate a `code_verifier` (43-128 character random string) and compute the `code_challenge`: ```javascript // Generate code_verifier const codeVerifier = base64URLEncode(crypto.randomBytes(32)); // Generate code_challenge (S256 method) const codeChallenge = base64URLEncode(sha256(codeVerifier)); ``` **Step 4: Authorization Request** Redirect the user to the authorization endpoint: ``` https://uithub.com/authorize? client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID& redirect_uri=https://your-app.com/callback& response_type=code& scope=read& state=RANDOM_STATE& code_challenge=YOUR_CODE_CHALLENGE& code_challenge_method=S256 ``` The user will authenticate with GitHub and be redirected back to your `redirect_uri` with an authorization `code`. **Step 5: Exchange Code for Access Token** ```bash curl -X POST https://uithub.com/token \ -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \ -d "grant_type=authorization_code" \ -d "code=AUTH_CODE_FROM_CALLBACK" \ -d "redirect_uri=https://your-app.com/callback" \ -d "client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID" \ -d "client_secret=YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET" \ -d "code_verifier=YOUR_CODE_VERIFIER" ``` Response: ```json { "access_token": "abc123...", "token_type": "Bearer", "expires_in": 86400, "scope": "read" } ``` **Step 6: Use the Access Token** Include the token in the Authorization header: ```bash curl https://uithub.com/owner/repo \ -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN" ``` ### Available Scopes - `read` - Read access to public repositories (default) - `repo` - Access to private repositories (requires GitHub repo scope) ### Supported OAuth Endpoints | Endpoint | Description | |----------|-------------| | `GET /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server` | OAuth server metadata | | `GET /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource` | Protected resource metadata | | `POST /register` | Dynamic client registration | | `GET /authorize` | Authorization endpoint | | `POST /token` | Token endpoint | ### Token Expiration - Authorization codes expire in **10 minutes** - Access tokens expire in **24 hours** ### PKCE Support PKCE is recommended for all clients. Supported methods: - `S256` (recommended) - SHA-256 hash of code_verifier - `plain` - code_verifier sent as-is

tags.name: OAuth
tags.description: OAuth 2.0 authentication endpoints for third-party applications

get.description:
Fetch contents of a GitHub repository, with various filtering options. 

Example usage:
- Max 10.000 LLM tokens in response: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search?maxTokens=10000
- Only markdown: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search?ext=md
- Only JS/TS: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search?ext=js,jsx,ts,tsx
- Respond in YAML: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search?accept=text/yaml 
- Max 10kb per file: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search?maxFileSize=10000 
- Glob include pattern: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search?include=**/*.ts,src/** 
- Glob exclude pattern: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search?exclude=**/node_modules/**,**/*.test.ts
- Search file contents: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search?search=function%20parse
- Case-sensitive search: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search?search=API&searchMatchCase=true
- Regex search: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search?search=export%20(const|function)&searchRegularExp=true

Known limitations: 

- Doesn't work well with large repos over 50mb
- For now the API is free to use for fair use, but a ratelimit will be added in the future.
- There is an hourly cap for githubs ratelimit, you can get up to 5000 requests per hour by using your own apiKey.

Read below for more the entire spec, or try it out in the widget and get some code.

Reach out [here](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=41797578) for feedback or feature requests.

200.description: A directory tree and all (filtered) files requested in the required format (html, json, text, or yaml).

402.description: Payment required - insufficient credit for private repository access

403.description: Forbidden - private repository access requires 'repo' scope

get.summary: Get contents relevant for an issue or discussion
get.description:
Fetch contents of a GitHub repository relevant for an issue or discussion, with various filtering options. 

Example usage:
- Max 10.000 LLM tokens in response: https://uithub.com/codefromanywhere/openapi-search/issues/1?maxTokens=10000
- Glob include pattern: https://uithub.com/owner/repo/issues/1?include=**/*.ts
- Search file contents: https://uithub.com/owner/repo/issues/1?search=function%20parse

Known limitations: 

- Doesn't work well with large repos over 50mb
- For now the API is free to use for fair use, but a ratelimit will be added in the future.
- There is an hourly cap for githubs ratelimit, you can get up to 5000 requests per hour by using your own apiKey.

Read below for more the entire spec, or try it out in the widget and get some code.

Reach out [here](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=41797578) for feedback or feature requests.

200.description: A directory tree and all (filtered) files requested in the required format (html, json, text, or yaml).

402.description: Payment required - insufficient credit for private repository access

403.description: Forbidden - private repository access requires 'repo' scope

get.description: Returns OAuth 2.0 authorization server metadata as defined in RFC 8414. Use this to discover OAuth endpoints and supported features.
Returns OAuth 2.0 protected resource metadata. Indicates the resource server and its associated authorization servers.

post.description: Dynamic client registration endpoint. Register a new OAuth 2.0 client to obtain client credentials.

get.description: Initiates the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow. Redirect users here to authenticate and authorize your application. Supports PKCE (code_challenge and code_challenge_method parameters).

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: The client ID obtained during registration

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: The URI to redirect to after authorization (must match registered URI)

parameters.name: response_type
parameters.description: Must be 'code' for authorization code flow

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Space-separated list of scopes. Available: 'read' (public repos), 'repo' (private repos)

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Random string to prevent CSRF attacks. Returned unchanged in the callback.

parameters.name: code_challenge
parameters.description: PKCE code challenge. Recommended for all clients.

302.description: Redirects to GitHub for authentication, then back to redirect_uri with authorization code

post.description: Exchange an authorization code for an access token. Supports PKCE verification.

bearerAuth.description: Bearer token obtained via OAuth 2.0 flow or API key from dashboard

oauth2.description: OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with PKCE support

scopes.repo: Access to private repositories (requires GitHub repo scope)

issuer.description: The authorization server's issuer identifier URL

registration_endpoint.description: URL of the dynamic client registration endpoint

ClientRegistrationRequest.description: Request body for OAuth client registration

redirect_uris.description: Array of allowed redirect URIs for the client

client_name.description: Human-readable name of the client application

token_endpoint_auth_method.description: Authentication method for the token endpoint

code.description: The authorization code received from the authorization endpoint

redirect_uri.description: Must match the redirect_uri used in the authorization request

code_verifier.description: PKCE code verifier (required if code_challenge was used in authorization)

access_token.description: The access token to use for API requests

tree.description: Token tree showing file/folder structure with token counts. Omitted if omitTree=true

files.description: Map of file paths to their contents. Omitted if omitFiles=true

SizeInfo.description: Size information about the repository contents

tokens.description: Number of tokens in the returned (filtered) content

totalTokens.description: Total number of tokens in the repository before filtering

characters.description: Number of characters in the content (totalTokens * 5)

lines.description: Total number of lines in the repository

TokenTree.description: Recursive tree structure where keys are file/folder names. Values are either numbers (token count for files) or nested TokenTree objects (for folders)

oneOf.description: Token count for a file (rounded to nearest 100)

FileContent.description: Content information for a single file

type.description: Type of file - 'content' for text files, 'binary' for binary files

url.description: URL to the file (only present for type='binary')

hash.description: SHA hash of the file content

size.description: Size of the file in bytes

yamlString.name: yamlString
yamlString.description: An URL Encoded string of a yaml of a subset of the file hierarchy of files you want to include

omitFiles.name: omitFiles
omitFiles.description: If set to 'true' the response will not include the files

omitTree.name: omitTree
omitTree.description: If set to 'true' the response will not include the tree. Only available when requesting result as data (json or yaml)

branch.name: branch
branch.description: Branch name (defaults to main if not provided)

path.name: path
path.description: File or directory path within the repository

apiKey.name: apiKey
apiKey.description: GitHub API key for private repositories. Make a token [here](https://github.com/settings/tokens/new)

ext.name: ext
ext.description: Comma-separated list of file extensions to include

dir.name: dir
dir.description: Comma-separated list of directories to include

excludeExt.name: exclude-ext
excludeExt.description: Comma-separated list of file extensions to exclude

excludeDir.name: exclude-dir
excludeDir.description: Comma-separated list of directories to exclude

disableGenignore.name: disableGenignore
disableGenignore.description:
`.genignore` works like `.gitignore` and the repo owner can place this file in the repo to automatically filter out these files from uithub.

To disable this automatic filter you can set this to true.

maxFileSize.name: maxFileSize
maxFileSize.description: Maximum file size to include (in bytes)

lines.name: lines
lines.description: Can be set to false to disable the line numbers before every line. Only needed for HTML or markdown responses.

maxTokens.name: maxTokens
maxTokens.description:
Useful for working with LLMs; Will remove big files from response to get under a certain token count. Will still include the file in the tree.
NB: as of now, this does not count the directory tree tokens, so actual tokens may be higher.

For HTML or Markdown requests this value defaults to 50000, otherwise infinite.

accept.name: accept
accept.description: Defaults to text/markdown. To receive JSON back, please use application/json

acceptQuery.name: accept
acceptQuery.description: Defaults to text/markdown. To receive JSON back, please use application/json

include.name: include
include.description: Comma-separated glob patterns for files to include (e.g., '**/*.ts,src/**'). Supports VS Code style patterns: * (single segment), ** (multiple segments), ? (single char), [abc] (char class), {a,b} (alternatives)

exclude.name: exclude
exclude.description: Comma-separated glob patterns for files to exclude (e.g., '**/node_modules/**,**/*.test.ts'). Supports VS Code style patterns: * (single segment), ** (multiple segments), ? (single char), [abc] (char class), {a,b} (alternatives)

search.name: search
search.description: Search string to filter files by content. Only files containing this text will be included in results.

searchMatchCase.name: searchMatchCase
searchMatchCase.description: Whether search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false (case-insensitive).

searchRegularExp.name: searchRegularExp
searchRegularExp.description: Whether the search string should be treated as a regular expression. Defaults to false (literal string search).
